candidates.stmt: Jeet Bains is your Councillor in Addiscombe East ward where he has lived for more than thirty years. Jeet has been a Councillor in Croydon since 2010 and takes a keen interest in helping local residents and businesses with any issues they have. Jeet is the Shadow Cabinet Member for Culture and Regeneration and has been a member of Scrutiny, Licensing and Pensions committees, as well as being the shadow Chair of the General Purposes and Audit Committee. By profession he is a management consultant, having helped organisations all over the world. Jeet's expertise in ensuring that the Council is run well is an invaluable asset. Please get in touch if you are a local resident or business - we will do all we can to help you.

I am an energy bills campaigner, environmentalist and new father. I live here in Addiscombe with my wife, daughter and two cats.

I am running to be a councillor because we have been let down time and again by Croydon's Conservative council.

During the last year, I set up an energy advice service to support residents with their energy bills, and successfully campaigned to save the bike parking at East Croydon station. As an engineer and former civil servant, I have the competence we need to turn around our failing council.

My partner and I are a recent addition to the Addiscombe East community, and we have been very happy with our move to the area with it’s lovely parks and relaxed high street. I am an active Conservative and I enjoy canvassing in the area, listening to residents on the doorstep about their hopes and concerns, which include the condition of the parks, Addiscombe High Street, traffic on residential roads, fly tipping and much else. If elected I will work to maintain and, where possible, improve conditions for all in Addiscombe East and Croydon as a whole.

As for me, I started my career in engineering project management and, following an MBA, I worked in corporate roles in the UK and abroad. I returned to the UK in 2010 and I now work on a part time basis for a couple of London businesses. This gives me the time and flexibility to represent you, and rest assured I will give 100% as your Ward councillor.

I was brought up in in Croydon and have lived here most of my life. I have worked in both the public and private sectors and have run my own business. I currently work for an advisory and financial services firm that is focused on helping business and capital accelerate the transition to net zero. Through my engagement in a variety of voluntary community roles, including helping long-term unemployed people with their CVs and job interview techniques; helping women new to this country to learn English; and working with a national charity tutoring disadvantaged GSCE pupils, I have demonstrated my commitment to serving the community. In Croydon I have been involved in a number of projects to improve our environment. I led work setting up the 'Bee World' garden at Heathfield House and helped fundraise for the Trees4Croydon scheme to get more trees planted in our streets and green spaces.

I have lived and worked in Croydon for over 20 years and know our borough’s strength, challenges and huge potential. As a secondary school teacher, I have dedicated my career to supporting young people, helping them fulfil their potential and grow into confident, responsible adults. Alongside this, I serve as a workplace union representative, with years of experience advocating for colleagues and standing up for fairness and accountability. I am passionate about Croydon and believe our community deserve strong committed representation. This election is our opportunity to choose positive positive change and unlock the future our borough deserves.

As an Independent candidate, I answer to the residents of Bensham Manor — not to a political party. I consider each issue carefully and vote in our community’s best interests. Every decision I take will be led by what is right for Bensham Manor.

Local decisions should reflect the people who live here — not party agendas.  Residents deserve a real say in decisions affecting their streets and services.

Rooted in Bensham Manor, I’ve been involved in local community work for years — long before this election. I see the challenges our area faces first-hand and step in when something isn’t right. I believe decisions affecting Bensham Manor should be shaped by local residents.

Already working hard means:
— Organising local clean-ups 
— Tackling fly-tipping and speeding up removals
— Challenging weak licensing decisions 
— Working to improve local parks
— Demanding transparency from the council

My priorities for Bensham Manor:
— Cleaner, safer streets with action on fly-tipping
— Practical action to reduce anti-social behaviour
— Improving our parks and green spaces
— A real say for residents in local decisions
— Transparency in council spending

Many residents tell me they are frustrated by party politics and decisions made without a real local voice.

As an Independent candidate, I take a different approach — focused on residents, practical action, and accountability.

If you want representation that listens first and answers to you, I would value your support.

Unlike all three 'Local" Conservative candidates, Ashley actually lives in the ward. Ashley Burridge has lived in Coulsdon since 2007. His wife Kate is a Palliative Care specialist nurse at Croydon University Hospital. Their teenage children went to Smitham Primary where Ashley chaired the PTA for several years. In his spare time he is a UK Athletics Technical Official, timekeeping and judging at track & field and cross country events across the south of England. Ashley has a wealth of experience in the building industry and currently chairs a small housing charity which looks after almshouses for senior residents. He understands the workings of local government having previously represented nearby Warlingham on Tandridge District Council for twelve years.

I will champion local voices and ensure residents’ concerns are heard.

Hello, I am standing to be your councillor in Broad Green because I want to continue making things better for all of us. I have lived in Croydon for over 20 years and there are so many things I love, including our strong community, but I know things need to change.

We need cleaner streets, safe spaces for everyone and a town centre we can be proud of again.

I previously worked as a SEND Teaching Assistant on London Road and supported organisations tackling youth violence through mentoring and educational programmes. I will make sure young people’s voices are heard in council decision making.

Working in a Business Improvement District to make our streets feel more welcoming and well run has taught me how to make real improvements happen. I want to work with residents, businesses, and partners to bring that same change to Broad Green.

I was born in Croydon and lived in a prefab in New Addington after the last War and watched it grow and evolve from a cold and desolate place with no roads or pavements and just 4 or 5 shops, into a thriving community, Croydon grew into a town to be proud of and almost made City status.

Unfortunately, things have changed for the worst and because of the appalling way that the 2 main parties have run the town for the last couple of decades we now have situation where the Borough has been bankrupt three times, leaving the town’s services poorly run with shops closing down and crime moving in.

I am frustrated with the daily news of illegal migration, along with violence, crime and a government who appears to lack the political will to deal with situation, similar things are happening to towns and cities all over our country.

Reform are brave and stand for freedom from the downward spiral of political and moral decline currently thriving in the UK.  We may not have all the answers but I know we will give it a damn good try to reverse the decline and put us back on a footing where we can work towards making our country a place to be proud of once again.

Titilope moved to Croydon with her family in 2016. She Holds B.Eng. Chemical Engineering, Certificate in Formulating Project Proposal for Climate Change Mitigation & Clean Energy, Certificate in Sustainable Environmental Management, Level II Domestic Retrofit, and Level 3 Energy Awareness.

She is a certified engineer and experienced Environmental Manager with 12 years of experience in climate change, renewable energy, emission management, and sustainable development working in the public sector.

Presently she is an Energy Advisor in the community sector because she is passionate about ensuring people are at the centre of the energy transition and believes that climate change and fuel poverty are both social justice issues and that they must be tackled together.

Titilope's priorities for Woodside include:

* ensuring residents get value for Council Tax paid; * promoting local job creation by encouraging apprenticeships in green jobs; * working with the Safer Neighborhood Team to make Woodside a safe place to live in; * promoting and helping build the confidence of young girls by promoting empowerment programs, seminars, and training in schools.

Ian Parker was a member of the Planning Committee regularly opposing Croydon Labour's inappropriate developments. Ian has lived and worked in the Coulsdon/Purley area for over 30 years He is married with two children both educated at local state schools and is the former Chair of Governors at Woodcote High School He was previously a Coulsdon Councillor (2010-2014) and Deputy Cabinet Member for Economic Development and Regeneration He is a former member of Coulsdon Forum and he has been a local volunteer for the Royal British Legion for over 20 years.

Having served as a Coulsdon West and then Coulsdon Town Councillor for seven years (2010-2014 and 2018-present) he has a clear picture of the role of a local Councillor. Key is the ability to listen and respond without promising that every wish can be met. As an ambassador for Coulsdon he seeks to promote the area to ensure the town centre remains a vibrant place to shop and work. The ever-growing developments provide challenges for local public services. Elected representatives need to ensure the services expand in line with the growing population. Any developments should preserve the green belt that should be cherished for ours and future generations. As a Conservative Councillor, he seeks to ensure we get value for money from our local taxes. To do that, we need a Conservative administration in the Town Hall. Coulsdon has for too long been neglected by a Labour Croydon Council.

Luke Shortland has lived in Croydon, moving between Coulsdon and Purley, for his entire life. He grew up attending a local Coulsdon nursery. One of the things Luke loves about Coulsdon is the real variety of shops in the town centre, with everything from a pottery café (where he got his first Saturday job over seven years ago!) to a candle shop. His main priority is to help Coulsdon be a town where local businesses can not only survive, but thrive. Luke also wants to make sure that new developments are appropriate and aesthetically pleasing - in stark contrast to the current Labour Council, who want to concrete over beautiful green spaces to build ugly-looking flats. Outside of work and politics, Luke is a bit of a foodie, and loves to travel. Luke’s also been learning German for the last few years, and residents are welcome to help him practise!

I’m representing Reform UK for Councillor in Coulsdon Town in the May 2026 by-election because I’m passionate about our nation and the ward I reside in. I’ve lived in the area for 19 years and together with my husband run a boiler maintenance business. Married for 34 years, we have two grown up children and are expecting our first grandchild at the end of March 2026.

On national issues, I have been heavily involved in the Anti ULEZ movement and in support of farmers by attending many of their rallies. Reform’s motto of Family, Community and Country will play a big part in any decisions made should I be elected. Being able to assist Coulsdon residents as one of Reform’s first councillors in Croydon will be a privilege. Together we can make the changes needed to keep improving the ward.

Coral M. Carroll: A Proven Leader. A Committed Neighbour. A Councillor Who Delivers.

Coral has over 15 years of experience across government, global business and community leadership. She brings a rare combination of practical expertise and genuine local commitment. She is standing for Old Coulsdon to deliver the expertise this ward deserves.

She has built policy that matters. She holds a Master's from the London School of Economics and began her career on the path to becoming a diplomat, working on economic policy after working in Westminster for an MP on education legislation to help children succeed.

She has served business interests. She has worked cross-sector with businesses large and small. She led digital strategy at the world's largest technology consulting firm before working with London fintech startups and founding her own Artificial Intelligence venture.

She is a community advocate. Coral built a grassroots women-in-technology organisation, where she helped thousands of women access skills and careers. Her work earned her distinguished accolades and awards, alongside participation at the World Economic Forum and audiences with Heads of State, championing women at the highest levels.

Since making South Croydon her home, Coral has wasted no time advocating for local issues. She serves as a Director of a local residents' association, has co-led efforts to hold a rogue local developer accountable, and successfully pushed the council to improve digital services for residents. A lover and protector of nature and green spaces, she spends her free time gardening with her young son and walking her dog through the local woodlands.

Coral is standing for Old Coulsdon because residents deserve a councillor who combines real expertise with genuine commitment to this neighbourhood — delivering practical results and putting the community first.

Rob has lived in Croydon for 14 years where he works as a local Driving Instructor and is standing as your Labour candidate in Coulsdon Town. He is also a Licensed Church of England Lay Minister and a volunteer tour guide at Shirley Windmill, a local historical attraction owned by Croydon Council.

Rob is proud to live in such a vibrant and diverse borough as Croydon, but is concerned about the increase in racism and intolerance, both in our local community and in wider society. Labour is committed to putting people first, regardless of race, gender or sexuality.

After four years of broken promises from Tory Mayor Jason Perry, Rob is proud to be standing alongside Rowenna Davis as Labour mayoral candidate who is dedicated to working for all people in order to restore pride in our great borough.

I’ve proudly called South Croydon home since 2012, having grown up near Glasgow before moving south to build my career in financial services. I’ve worked in banking and insurance, led major projects, and more recently in Westminster supporting an MP, helping residents with casework and contributing to national policy. Throughout my career, one principle has guided me: putting people first.

Coulsdon has enormous potential, but too many residents feel Croydon Council simply is not delivering. From potholes and fly-tipping to children’s services and community safety, local people deserve better. As your councillor, I will focus on practical action:

•	Cleaner streets 
•	Safer neighbourhoods 
•	Reliable local services 
•	Opportunities for people of all ages to work, learn and thrive

Across the borough, people can see that Croydon Town Centre has been allowed to decline, with boarded-up shops, empty office blocks, closing businesses and the recent loss of the town centre cinema. If you are happy with that, then vote for anyone. But if you want real change, fresh energy and new life in our town centre, then it is time to vote Labour.

I am proud to support Rowenna Davis for Mayor of Croydon. Rowenna is a teacher, a mum, and someone who understands the pressures families face every day. She has a clear plan to rebuild trust in the Council, restore pride in our borough, fix the finances, support safer streets and bring jobs, investment and visitors back to Croydon. You can read more at: rowennadavis.co.uk/mymanifesto.

I also believe young people must have a real voice in shaping our future. I helped establish a youth democracy charity and served as its first Chair, because every generation deserves a stake in the decisions that affect them.
Together, with Rowenna Davis and a strong Labour team, we can restore confidence in our Council, revive our town centre, and once again put people first.

Promoted by Stuart King on behalf of Rowenna Davis and Ben Taylor on behalf of

I was delighted to be elected as the Liberal Democrat Councillor for Crystal Palace and Upper Norwood in 2022. Since then I have worked hard to represent my residents, completing over 600 pieces of casework and campaigning on reversing bus cuts, improving parks and opposing the 33% rise in council tax since 2022.

Outside of politics I am the Chief Executive of Sculpt, a london-based youth charity and have over 25 years experience in the charity sector supporting young people, those affected by poverty and homelessness and victims of crime.

Natalie Vesty is standing for the first time as a Green Party candidate in the Crystal Palace and Upper Norwood ward.

Natalie says: "I'm a choir leader and love working with other people and helping them to achieve their goals. I realised this skill sat wonderfully with being a councillor! I also grew up in a very multicultural area, and it saddens me to see how divided we are becoming as a society. I want to be part of bringing people together, and running for a councillor seems like an excellent way to do that. I have been living in Crystal Palace for 8 years, and I love this community. I believe I can really help the residents of Crystal Palace and Upper Norwood."

I am standing to be a candidate in the wonderful Crystal Palace & Upper Norwood ward not for personal gain or kudos, not for political aspirations, but from a strong desire to make a real difference to the community.

For 11 years I was on the committee of the local Marie Curie Charity and then for 4 years I was Chairman of the RSPCA in South London

I designed and still run the websites for our church on Westow Street and a local Labour Party

Now my focus is on helping residents, community groups & businesses to deal with their issues, champion their ideas and alleviate their concerns.

Recently I have had many dozens of fly-tips removed, have secured funding for the paths of Upper Norwood Rec to be repaired, started a big campaign to get Croydon Council to clean up & maintain Beulah Hill Pond and another to clear the horrid fly-tip behind Victory Place off the Triangle.

I have also helped multiple residents have issues fixed in their homes & on their streets.

After a long career in finance & payments, plus expertise in charity & institution governance, I am now semi-retired and ready to bring my business acumen and expert organisational skills to give back to a community I love.

I am here to serve you, not inflate my ego or impress my friends.

I’m standing to be a councillor who’s visible, approachable, and firmly on the side of local people.

Crystal Palace & Upper Norwood has a unique, independent spirit — and I want to protect and strengthen that. From supporting our brilliant high streets and local businesses to keeping our streets clean and safe, I’ll focus on the things that matter day to day.

I believe good councillors listen and act. That means being out in the community, taking concerns seriously, and following through to get results.

Working with a Labour government, we have a real opportunity to bring more investment into our area — and I’ll make sure our community benefits.

If elected, I’ll be a strong voice for residents and a councillor you can rely on.

I am 72 years old, was born in Camberwell, grew up in East Dulwich, moved to Croydon around 45 years ago.

Worked from age 16 to 60 years old, secretarial and administration roles, one of which was as secretary to the Head Postmaster of Croydon. I am married to Roger and am now his main carer. I enjoy travelling and socialising with friends, gardening and chatting with neighbours.

I would like to see our area of London cleaned up and restored to how I remember it some years ago.

I stood to be a councillor in 2022 because I was fed up of seeing systems fail the people who need them most. But over the last 4 years I’ve seen just how deep those failures go. There’s been far too many ways the council has failed people, sometimes with devastating impact.

I care deeply about people - whether I represent you, or not; whether you vote for me or not - and I work hard, sometimes fiercely advocating for residents and community organisations. But I shouldn’t have to - at least not for residents to have the bare minimum: communication.

Politicians need to dream bigger for Croydon, but that vision should start with getting the basics right at the council first. I want Croydon to have a caring council, and one that that puts power back into residents’ hands. I know only the Greens will deliver that.

Both Labour and the Conservatives have failed Croydon, bankrupting the council, having poor management and making bad decisions. Now bills continue to rise, and residents are paying 33% more in council tax, for less services – not even remotely touching the council's debt. Greens have consistently voted against council tax increases, and put forward budget amendments showing that some services didn’t need to be cut. We’ve advocated that the government needs to intervene when it comes to Croydon's debt – and now have the establishment parties agreeing.

Croydon is a borough with so much potential. I was born and raised in Croydon, and I live in Fairfield. I see the issues in the town centre every day, and I’m determined to bring it back to life and tidy up our neighbourhoods.

As a councillor I’ve enjoyed working alongside residents and campaigners to get their issues heard and resolved, particularly on housing, equality and environmental issues. You can find out more about what we’ve delivered on https://croydon.greenparty.org.uk/fairfield/ We listen to residents and actually take action.

With your vote and with more Greens, we can get more done – and ther

I'm standing because I am angry with the way both Labour and the Conservatives have squandered the chance to improve the lives of Croydon residents on failed projects that have left the town bankrupt. Croydon has so much potential, but it is being held back by clueless politicians and corporate interests who only want to make a quick profit.
My experience as a senior councillor in another authority and as a senior manager in the public sector has convinced me that it does not have to be this way. We can rebuild our communities by working together, putting residents at the heart of decisions, and making council spending accountable to you.

I am standing for Reform UK because I believe Croydon’s future depends on practical leadership, financial responsibility, and restoring trust in local government. Having lived in the borough for many years, I’ve seen first-hand the challenges residents face — from high council tax to declining public services — and I want to be part of the solution. My goal is to ensure that every pound spent by the council delivers real value to our community.

Professionally, I work in technology and data-driven problem-solving, where clarity, accountability, and results matter. These are values I want to bring into local decision-making. I am committed to cutting waste, improving service delivery, and championing a more transparent, resident-focused council. Croydon deserves strong, competent representation, and I want to use my experience and determination to deliver meaningful improvements for Fairfield and our wider community.

I have lived in Kenley for the past fourteen years and have been a Kenley Councillor since 2022. Kenley is a beautiful and special place, and one I know well — I regularly walk the family dog through its green spaces and feel proud to call it home.

I am committed to representing residents and championing our local area. I work closely with residents, community groups, council officers and external partners to address the issues that matter most to our community and to safeguard Kenley’s unique character and environment. I advocate for residents on council matters, campaign for improved transport connections, and raise important local concerns with the council and relevant agencies.

I have facilitated key improvements for Kenley residents, including a new zebra crossing over the A22, a bus service to ‘upper’ Kenley, action on flooding hotspots, and championing play for younger residents

Good local decision-making begins with listening. I strive to be accessible, responsive, and practical in my approach, ensuring that residents’ voices are clearly heard and respected.

In my profession, I work within the geographic information industry, using spatial data for better decision making.

I live in Kenley with my wife Fiona and our two-year-old daughter Chloe. We love Kenley and have chosen to make it our home for our young family.

I’ve been a committed community activist for my entire working life. I’m proud to have spent a large amount of my career leading with lots of different groups to promote community cohesion, particularly among young people. For me, nothing is more important than building a strong, passionate community. If I’m lucky enough to be elected as the next Councillor for Kenley, that’s what you’ll get from me 24 hours a day.

That’s what I think Kenley needs. We’re full of so many incredible local groups run by committed local people. I want to work with you all to ensure you get the support that you need from Croydon Council.

I am a Ward Champion for Reform in Kenley because I am fed up.

We badly need to be governed by people who care about the country’s future. At the very least, that means keeping us safe, defending our borders, protecting our institutions and respecting our history. Labour is doing none of those things. The Tories are once again talking conservative but they have shown repeatedly that they cannot be trusted in power.

Between them, the established parties have also driven the economy into the ground, by destroying incentives and losing control of spending, locally as well as nationally.

I am hopeful for the future too.

Reform has put together a programme to put things right, including radical legislation to restore the sovereignty of parliament and start rebuilding Britain as a prosperous, multi-ethnic but culturally coherent, nation-state, based on mutual respect and love of country.

We have exciting plans – set out in “Croydon Needs Reform: Our Contract with You” – to begin this process after the local elections in May.

I was brought up in Essex and spent a long career in research and teaching. My home has been Purley for the last 25 years and I spend much of my leisure time in Kenley. The common, and the airfield and its traditions, are close to my heart.

I shall be proud to represent the interests of Kenley ward.

Kacper is an 18-year-old student who has lived in Croydon for almost his entire life. He has seen first-hand the challenges many residents face, including crime, fly-tipping, and poor accessibility in parts of the borough. These issues are deeply personal to him: as a wheelchair user, he understands how inaccessible streets, transport, and public spaces can directly affect people’s independence and quality of life.

He believes Kenley is too often overlooked, yet many challenges are emerging - vehicle theft is rising, and many residents feel
isolated. Kacper recognises residents concerns and plans to act on them.

I am your local resident and community activist standing up on the platform of the Labour Party to contest for Councillor, New Addington North in the upcoming elections of Thursday 7th May 2026.
New Addington is my home and have lived here for over 15 years.
I have been the Councillor representing New Addington North since 2021 and for my day job, I work at Kingston University 
I am a firm believer in community values, enabling everyone's voice to be heard and justice for all, no matter your background or where you live. 
I am thrilled that the Labour Government has awarded New Addington £20m Pride in Place funding. I will work with the Independent Pride in Place panel and people across the area to ensure that residents’ priorities are met and that this investment delivers real improvements for everyone. 
I promise to put People First.
Croydon is fast becoming a ghost town but you can help us save it by voting all Labour and Cooperative candidates on 7th May

I am a proud New Addington resident and Community Advocate.
I have lived here for many years, working closely with local families and understanding the issues that matter in most to residents.
I am standing to champion Safer Streets, Stronger Services and a brighter future for New Addington.
I promise to put People First.
I am thrilled that this Labour government has awarded New Addington £20m Pride in Place funding. I will work with the Independent Panel and people from across New Addington to ensure that Residents’ priorities are met and that this investment delivers real improvements for everyone.
Croydon is becoming a ghost town but you can help us save it by voting all Labour and Cooperative candidates on 7th May.

I am running because we can bring much-needed change not only to our borough, but to the country as a whole.

My career to date has involved policing, security, and education (as well as running businesses), so I can see how various elements need to flourish if our nation is to prosper.

I intend to foster a secure, confident, and stable future for our children by tackling head-on the problems we face in a decisive but fair manner.

Kelsey is a first year student at the University of Oxford, and has a background in environmental and social justice campaigning. Kelsey is passionate about ensuring that Oxford is a city with a strong and thriving local democracy that places people and the planet are put first. As a disabled young person, Kelsey is dedicated to seeing inclusion and representation at all levels.

I am proud to have called New Addington my home for the last 15 years. My husband has been here for 40 and his family since the new estate was built. I love the sense of community here. This is our own little town and I’m really proud of it. When I’m on my way back from my shifts I get that sense of being ‘home’, not when I get inside my door but when I step off the tram.

I have been a part of the wider New Addington community for many years now. I am a committee member for the New Addington Royal British Legion and more recently was one of the founding members of the much-needed New Addington Residents’ Association.

I am all about our community. Someone once told me ‘You have ADDO at heart’. I am passionate about New Addington and believe that the people of New Addington North deserve to be heard.

Tony has been active locally for many years and was the Conservative Candidate for New Addington in 2006 before serving as New Addington Councillor from May 2010 to 2014. He is married to Maria and lives in Queen Elizabeth's Drive, New Addington. His family have always had links with New Addington, with many members of his extended family still living in the area. He is a former Commanding Officer of the local NTC unit and was also Chair of Governors at Selsdon High, Vice Chair of Governors at Rowdown Primary School. Tony is a former member of the Reguar Army and supports Help for Heroes and SSAFA, and is a former Chairman of New Addington Royal British Legion. Tony has worked in Funeral Services as Operations Manager for a national Funeral Company, and is now Funeral Arranger and Conductor for a local family run funeral company and am based in New Addington.

I have proudly lived in Croydon for over 30 years and raised my family here. I love New Addington for its green spaces and peaceful surroundings. As a Support Housing Manager, I work closely with residents and understand the challenges they face. I witness on a daily basis how council decisions can impact families; this is why I am standing as a candidate in  New Addington South. I would like to expand youth services, working with local charities to give young people safe places to go.

I have lived in New Addington for 20 years. I  made it my home because of the beautiful green spaces  and good transport links with the  tram.I  am standing as a Labour and Co -operative candidate because I believe that our community deserves better representation and a stronger voice. As a local resident, I understand the challenges we face, like anti-social behaviour and fly-tipping. I am passionate about working with residents, businesses and community groups to build  a brighter future for our community.

- Have lived in Norbury for over 15 years 
- Local mother 
- Advocate for fair accessible SEND and alternative educational provisions
- Adult and Paediatric Special Care Dentist in the NHS for 20 years
- Previously a University of London Lecturer
- Anti-racist/anti-colonial/anti-imperialist

I am standing because I believe in **People Over Profit**.
Its time we stop looking to billionaire corporates to solve our housing, health, energy and mental health crisis; they have been the beneficiaries of a system tipped against ordinary working people for too long.
**For true _people-led transparent democracy_, invested in local community building, where our children and grandchildren have a safe. clean and positive future, VOTE GREEN**

I am proud to stand for Norbury Park Ward as someone who has been part of this community for over 22 years. Through my local corner shop, I have supported residents, listened to your concerns, and build strong relationships with families and local businesses.

I believe in working together to create safer streets, cleaner neighbourhoods, and real opportunities for growth, I am passionate about supporting small businesses and helping our local economy thrive. My commitment is to continue serving, supporting, and strengthening the community we all call home.

It is with pride that I am standing in Norbury Park ward, which has an incredible community with some amazing green spaces such as Norbury Park itself, Norwood Grove and Biggin Wood. Alongside making our streets safer, cleaner and supporting the local economy to grow, I am also keen to protect our green spaces. I have lived in the London Borough of Croydon all my life, I was born in May Day Hospital and I have been to school, college and I have worked in this borough. It will always be my home and I am committed to supporting local residents to protect and improve this ward.

Don Charles-Lambert is standing to be YOUR Councillor in Crystal Palace & Upper Norwood. He's lived in Upper Norwood since he was 2 years old, and even attended Downsview Primary & Nursery School! He has a strong professional and educational background having studied Computer Science at UCL, Economics at Cambridge and worked as an Analyst at J.P. Morgan. He hopes to transfer this experience to the Council, where his main priorities are to restore our Borough's abysmal finances, re-open the roads and clean up the streets.

Received a South African Matric (school leaving certificate) at Belgravia Commercial High School and one and a half years of a three year diploma with The Association of Commercial &  Financial Technicians of Southern Africa in conjunction with Witwatersrand Technikon.

My working life, spent in the capacities ranging from Secretarial, Company Secretarial, Administration. Share Trust Administration and Governance, spanned various office positions from starting my career as a shorthand typist in the South African Railways upon leaving school to company secretarial governance and share trust administration with the ability to build strong relations with all stakeholders.  With approximately 26 years of experience in various business entities including auditing, asset management, corporate law, CIB banking, fast moving consumer goods, share trusts and various niche markets.

Was given British Citizenship upon marrying my Liverpool born husband in 1972.  Immigrated to UK in 2014. Subsequent to arriving in UK it seemed prudent to ensure that I had acceptable British qualifications to offer, if needed, so undertook and received a level 3 hairdressing NVQ qualification from Croydon College.  Although South African born my heritage was and is predominantly British in family culture.  My family love Britain and all my immediate family live in UK.

It is my absolute resolve and intentional delight to if elected serve the residents of Norbury Park ward in accordance with the mandate of Reform UK’s Croydon comprehensive set of policies designed to deliver a safer, more prosperous, and resident-focused future for the borough ensuring urgent practical solutions, fiscal discipline, and community empowerment.

Reform Croydon aims to restore pride in our communities, boost our economy, and ensure every resident’s voice shapes our borough’s destiny.
Reform pledges to, inter alia, tackle crime, fix the finances, protect the Green Belt, reform local services, and get Croydon moving.

Born in Mayday Hospital, I’m a lifelong Croydonian with deep roots in our community. For over 20 years, I have served as a school governor at schools across the borough. I ran the Vote Leave campaign in Croydon and have stood for office before.

I had hoped to leave party politics behind, but I’ve decided to run because I remember growing up in an area full of prosperity and opportunity – with excellent shopping, strong road networks, and a vibrant nightlife. Years of failure and political meddling by the council have squandered much of that legacy, leaving taxpayers with mounting debt while hollowing out vital services.

I want to help deliver a council that gets the basics right and empowers the people of Croydon to build the local businesses, jobs, and services we all desire.

With a MA in history from the University of Cambridge, I spent a 40 career in the shipping industry and almost as many as a resident of the Croydon area. Working in the City on the Executive Committee of the Board of Directors, I worked extensively overseas in Tokyo for over 3 years, in Singapore as CEO for over 3 years and in Dubai as a COO for over 5 years all as bases to do business around the world.  Advising large multinational companies on global shipping strategy, I have been involved in thousands of deals.

I have seen how countries in Asia have overtaken Europe and Britain by having sensible policies run by professionals and how the opposite has happened in Britain. I will bring commercial rigour into local government so taxpayer money is not wasted and more can be retained by voters/taxpayers – the way more prosperous societies are created.

I have also authored books on how shipping works and after retirement a wrote a three volume history of maritime trade, the last of which The Ascent of Maritime Trade 1700-2025 (due out in April 2026) includes a chapter analysing the decline of maritime Britain and hence Britain itself – described by a peer reviewer as ‘an essential text for today’. For more on my books and social media output please go to https://maritimetradehistory.com/.

I was born in Clapham and spent my early years in Streatham. I have been a resident in the Coulsdon/Purley area for the last 46 years and married for 52 years with two grown up children & 4 grandchildren.

Recently retired I was a Consulting Engineer in the construction industry and ran my own Consultancy Practice for 38 years based in Sutton and latterly in the borough of Croydon.

I have watched from the sidelines with dismay the gradual decline of the borough of Croydon under various administrations decaying from a forward-looking modern town centre built largely in the 60’s, to be reduced to an indebted, shadow of its former glory as a thriving centre of commerce.  The incompetence of previous administrations has led to vast debts which will probably never be repaid, leaving local council taxpayers on the hook for significant debt repayments far into the future.

I am now, both angered and saddened by the state of the borough and I have reached to point at which I feel I have to do something to arrest the decline and be an active participant in rebuilding the borough. I believe a Reform administration can make a material difference to the direction Croydon must take.

I now have the time and energy to put something back into Croydon and hitherto under the two-party system, saw nothing to choose between them. I have been inspired by the advent of a 3rd force in politics, with an agenda for real change and a break with the past.

The new energy that is apparent, both in the new party and the population at large will enable us to inject new perspectives into the debate and have a material impact on people’s lives in the borough.

Hi all.My name is Dora Makrogianni  and I m a candidate  for Old Coulsdon  council. I m a waitress  at Los Arcos  in Coulsdon  so some of you maybe know me already. I reassure  you for this,I ll make my best for Old Coulsdon s residents  and I ll.never give up for defending  the values of democracy.

I'm proud to be your Local Conservative Candidate for Park Hill & Whitgift on the 2nd May.

I have lived in Croydon my whole life, and I chose to make Park Hill my home 11 years ago. I love the sense of community that exists in this part of Croydon - that’s why my wife and I have chosen to raise our two children here.

I am a criminal barrister, and am known for standing up for those I represent. Outside of work, I tend an allotment in Park Hill, as well as being a regular runner in the Lloyd Parkrun. I attend Croydon Redeemer Church at Archbishop Tenison’s, and I’m a proud Crystal Palace Season Ticket holder.

From 2006-2010, I served as a Councillor for the nearby Addiscombe ward, during which time I: • Helped the development and opening of Addiscombe Railway Park, • Established a Conservation Area to help prevent the overdevelopment of flats, and • Represented residents at a major public inquiry to defeat a large inappropriate development.

Vote for the Local Conservatives on Thursday 2nd May, so that we can continue the progress made by Conservative Mayor Jason Perry to fix Croydon’s finances, to change Labour’s planning policies, and to get Croydon Town Centre moving again.

**I’m standing to be re-elected as a councillor for Purley Oaks & Riddlesdown, the ward I currently represent and the ward I have lived in since 2016.**

Professionally, I’ve ran my own business, and worked in both the private sector and for charities - including a charity based in Croydon helping residents here.

Over the last four years, I’ve been privileged to represent the Purley Oaks and Riddlesdown ward, and focus on delivering residents priorities:

1. A planning system that delivers the right new development in the right places - and protecting the character of our neighbourhoods, by stopping the replacement of family homes with oversized blocks of flats
2. Championing new investment and development, and working with the developers to bring a new Marks & Spencer store to Brighton Road that will open in 2027
3. Basic services that work for everyone - ensuring our grass verges and green spaces are cut and maintained; that our roads and pavements are clean; and our roads are repaired and resurfaced.
4. Engaging with our Met Police Safer Neighbourhood Team for the ward, ensuring they are aware of issues that residents are reporting
5. Supporting our independent small businesses and working with our local Residents Associations to support their priorities

Between 2022-2024, I served as Deputy Cabinet Member for Finance, and I’m proud to have done my part in helping to get Croydon’s finances back on track under Mayor Perry. In 2024, I moved to a scrutiny role, chairing our Streets & Environment sub-committee and as Vice-chair of the Scrutiny & Overview Committee: ensuring that the decisions the council are taking are sound and appropriate.

For the last four years, I’ve also served on the council’s Pension Committee, ensuring our £2bn+ pension fund is well invested and managed, to deliver the retirement benefits that pension scheme members expect in return for their and their employer’s contributions.

Most importantly, I’ve delivered hundreds of pieces of casewo

I'll get things done for local residents, for more than two decades I’ve worked at the heart of Government, including in Health, Defence (housing), and the National Crime Agency, where cutting through bureaucracy is essential, I know how to fix problems and deliver outcomes that matter.

Purley Oaks and Riddlesdown is blessed with beautiful green spaces, excellent schools and a strong local business community. But residents also face challenges from persistent fly tipping around Riddlesdown and Sanderstead Station Parade, to anti social behaviour and increasing rat running through residential streets.

As a councillor, I would focus on these day to day issues: keeping our neighbourhood clean and safe, working with enforcement teams, and making sure residents’ concerns are acted on. I want to champion the area’s potential supporting independent businesses, pop-up shops and exploring opportunities for creative spaces. But this can only succeed as part of a joined up plan for the whole of Croydon, where local voices shape local priorities. Which is why I am proud to support Rowenna Davis as candidate for Croydon Mayor. Rowenna has a fantastic manifesto which can deliver real change and hope for Croydon.

A great councillor gets things done and solves problems for their community. The public sector can often be confusing and bureaucratic. I know what needs to be done to make things happen.

I believe in democracy and I am asking you to trust me in this privileged position so that I can help change for the better.

I have been a small-time landlord and developer, a train conductor, a train driver, a bus driver amongst other jobs. I have enjoyed meeting people and discussed our woes, likes and desires and I feel that this position would allow me to influence some decision made on behalf of the people living in Croydon. If elected I would like to get value for money in spending, try to reduce stress in people’s lives, make payments for parking more accessible and increase public seating.

As a confident councillor with common-sense and a doctrine of following rules, regulations and doing the right thing, I am asking you to trust me in this position to stand up for real people with real lives.

Let’s make 2026 a good year together.

I am standing for election in Sanderstead because our community deserves a councillor who will stand up for local residents, protect what makes this area special, and ensure we receive the services we pay for. For too long, Croydon residents have been asked to pay more and accept less: soaring council tax, crumbling roads and pavements, unreliable waste collection, and a Town Hall that often fails to listen.

Sanderstead contributes heavily to the borough, yet too often we are taken for granted. That must change.

I believe in competence, accountability and value for money, and I will work tirelessly to make sure Sanderstead gets its fair share. I will campaign for proper investment in our roads and pavements, stronger action on crime and anti-social behaviour, and real protection for our green spaces from inappropriate development.

This campaign is about restoring pride, common sense and trust in local government. Residents deserve clean streets, safer neighbourhoods and better local services. I will fight for improved bus and transport links, more effective street cleaning, tougher action on fly-tipping and littering, and greater police visibility so people feel safe in every part of our ward.

I will also challenge waste and mismanagement wherever I find it. Croydon cannot continue to spend more while delivering less. Every pound of taxpayers’ money should be spent wisely and transparently, with residents’ priorities coming first.

On a personal level, I bring extensive senior-level experience of cutting through public sector waste and bureaucracy, helping local businesses grow and building strong relationships with many of London’s key organisations. That experience gives me the knowledge, determination and contacts to get things done and deliver practical results for Sanderstead.

I will work closely with residents, schools, community groups and the police to tackle local problems and find practical solutions. Above all, I will be an independent-minded, hardwo

Laura lives locally, shops locally and walks her dogs locally. Daily, she experiences and hears the challenges and see the issues that affect us all. Laura is committed to ensuring our local environment is fit for all residents, and will be a vocal advocate for their needs. Principally Laura wants clearer and safer streets, and a town centre we can all be proud of.

Alan and his family have been Sanderstead residents for over 35 years.  He was a school inspector and a senior Education Officer for Croydon Council. Alan is a strong advocate for Croydon’s open spaces. Locally he takes an interest in neighbourhood crime, public transport and planning matters; he is committed to maintaining Sanderstead’s attractive street scene.

Mike is proud to stand alongside his fellow Labour candidates in Sanderstead. He will prioritise practical change—improving local services, making streets safer, protecting green spaces, and strengthening the local economy with responsible development and thriving shops. Listening to residents and working with the community, Mike will champion a fairer, greener, and more inclusive Sanderstead for all.

Unlike all three "Local" Conservative candidates, Frances actually lives in the ward. Frances Conn has lived in Coulsdon for many years and runs a highly successful accountancy practice from her home. She has stood for election to the council on previous occasions as she is passionate about keeping our town from becoming over-developed and spoilt by developers. Frances volunteers with the Motor Neurone Disease Association, providing support for patients who need someone to empathise with them. In her spare time she enjoys the company of local Ramblers’ groups, taking time to appreciate our beautiful area.

I am proud to serve as your local councillor for Selhurst and to represent our community with commitment, fairness and strong local leadership. I live in Selhurst and have done so since 2003, meaning I have lived here for over 20 years. I see and experience the same issues residents face every day, and that drives me to act.

Selhurst is a place I care deeply about. My approach is rooted in a people first mindset. Every decision I make starts with what is best for local residents and our community. I work to make sure your concerns are heard and acted on, whether that is tackling fly-tipping, improving street cleanliness, addressing housing issues, or pushing for safer neighbourhoods.

My approach is simple: listen carefully, act decisively, and follow through.

My priorities for our community are:

* Cleaner, safer streets with tougher action on fly-tipping and anti-social behaviour
* Better housing standards and faster responses to repairs and disrepair
* Stronger community safety, working closely with local police and partners
* Practical support for residents facing cost of living pressures
* Continued investment in Selhurst so our area improves for everyone

I bring experience from my work in public service, including supporting residents with complex needs and helping people build confidence and skills through my role as a driving instructor. This has shaped my understanding of the real challenges people face and the importance of clear, practical solutions.

What you can expect from me:

1) To be approachable and available when you need support
2) To take action and keep you updated on progress
3) To stand up for Selhurst and ensure our community gets its fair share

I am committed to continuing to work hard for you and to delivering real results that make a difference to everyday life.

If re-elected, I will continue to put residents first and work tirelessly to build a cleaner, safer and stronger Selhurst.

Hello, my name is Brian. I am an English Christian who has lived in Selhurst since 2011 and married a Nigerian woman in South Norwood in 2015. I am a law graduate and a qualified counsellor.

I am standing for Reform to represent and serve residents of Selhurst in the 2026 local elections. Reforming Croydon Council cannot wait. Croydon Council too often doesn’t get the basics right, for instance the website croydon.gov.uk remains much it was when originally registered.  It is close to unusable for many residents.

I will work with Council Cabinet members and staff to redesign a functional and modern website that is accessible and effective for Croydon residents and visitors. I have personal experience in using Croydon Council’s housing services and dispute resolution procedures.  My professional life has included automation of financial services contracts, reducing costs by 30% and ensuring absolute data integrity.

Croydon Council needs to be reformed to work for residents.

Elect me, Brian Putman for Reform UK to represent the residents of Selhurst Ward in May 2026.

I have lived in the local area for the past 41 years, including the last 13 years in Selsdon, where I live with my partner and our two children. I have seen first-hand the decline of our local communities and understand how poor decisions made by Labour and the Conservatives have affected everyday life for residents.

For too long, people have felt ignored while businesses have been overtaxed, councils mismanaged, and communities allowed to deteriorate. I believe local people deserve to have their voices heard and their concerns properly represented.

I want to help return power to residents by standing up for the issues that affect their day-to-day lives – from council accountability and housing to local services, community safety, and fairness. My goal is to restore pride in our area, support local businesses, and ensure decisions are made with honesty, transparency, and common sense.

Reform offers a real alternative to the failed politics of the past, putting local people first and delivering the change our community and country needs.

I have lived and worked in croydon for 30 years and watched as the established political parties have let down local people with poor policies and increased our council tax.If elected i will put Community needs front and centre with common sense,a can do attitude,hard work and empathy being my watch words.I will Seek more bang for your buck and fight for the reasons for the councils budget statement being 50% redacted,thats £316 million of your money spent and unexplained.Reform will offer a fresh approach to the problems and opportunities our borough faces after the mismanagement by both Labour and the Tories.
Graeme Stobart Reform UK
Vote Refom get Reform
Thank You

Unlike all three "Local" Conservative candidates, Andy actually lives in the ward. Andy Sparkes has lived in the ward for eighteen years and is married with two children who attended Smitham Primary and Oasis Acadamy. He had a 31 year career in the railway industry in various commercial roles culminating as Business Development Director for Virgin Trains. Now retired from the railway industry, he has become a part-time forester and has an active life volunteering on the Bluebell Railway and as secretary of the local allotment society. Andy also helps with the 1st Coulsdon Scouts. He would bring a wealth of business experience and a passion in transport planning to the role.

My name is Jack Barwell. I'm a 23-year-old first-time Croydon candidate standing in Selsdon Vale and Forestdale. I'm running because I think we need a new generation of politicians to get involved and help build on the work the mayor and our Council have done to turn things around in the last 4 years. But also to deliver a future for young people and blue collar workers and deliver hope to those who have lost it in this great Town.

I've lived in Croydon all my life and from my youth to the present I've always tried to get back to the community. When I was in sixth form, I developed a public speaking competition and helped train up hundreds of young students at different schools in Croydon in both primary and secondary school to develop their vocal skills and public speaking.

When I was at university I founded my own charity Bridge The Gap to help state school students get into top universities by training them up for interviews, entrance exams, and personal statements. My charity helped hundreds of students apply for university and we have many success stories of those who got into Oxbridge.

In my spare time, I love watching sport and can't wait to cheer on England at the world cup this summer. You can also often find me at one of the great pubs or restaurants in Selsdon Vale and Forestdale whether that be the Sir Julian Huxley, the Kingsmen, the Forestdale forum or the Royal Tandoori. We all need to support our businesses at this difficult economic time when they're being overtaxed and underappreciated..

Peter has lived in Croydon for over twenty years and is very active in his local community. He is on the committee of his Residents' Association and his local woodland Friends group. He is a former chair of Croydon Friends of the Earth and was also a member of the Croydon Climate Crisis Commission. Peter was brought up in the midlands of England, the son and grandson of coal miners and car factory workers. Peter went to his local comprehensive school and, despite becoming homeless when he was 17 years old, successfully gained a place at Oxford University where he studied Philosophy and Psychology. Peter also has a Master's degree in Business Administration. Peter spent the first half of his career working in the civil service. He started off offering benefits advice in Croydon and over the years moved through many different government roles and promotions to end up leading the team that wrote the UK's renewable energy strategy. He has worked in both the UK Parliament and the European Parliament. For the last ten years Peter has worked for environment conservation charities, working to support volunteers looking after our local parks and woodlands as well as working on large rewilding projects in Scotland. Peter has been active in the Green Party for over ten years and has held roles at local and national level in the party, as well as representing the Green Party of England and Wales at the European level.

A warm welcome, and thank you for taking the time to read this.

I was born in Croydon in 1975 and have lived and worked in Croydon and London all my life. Over the years, I have witnessed significant changes to our town — from the rise of HMOs, high-density developments, and temporary accommodation, to shifts in how our neighbourhoods feel and function. Like many residents, I have invested heavily in my home and remain deeply invested in the future of Croydon.

By background, I was fortunate to work as a professional chorister, including with the Royal School of Church Music under Martin Howe. Today, I hold senior roles managing global positions for technology companies, giving me experience of how communities, businesses, and governments succeed — and where they fall short — across different countries and cultures.

It is with genuine concern that I feel our great nation has slipped behind many others in recent years. My family roots and my entire life in Croydon have shaped a strong belief in standing up for what is right, protecting our communities, and planning responsibly for the future.

I was born at Mayday Hospital, attended school in Croydon, and studied for my A levels at Selsdon Library. I have volunteered in a primary school and worked with children's groups. Together, these experiences have shown me a cross-section of our community.

I am hopeful that we can build a better Croydon. We are a borough with so much potential. My decision to stand as a candidate is rooted in my belief in Rowenna. Last summer, I spent three months shadowing her, seeing the breadth of our borough. It was both a privilege and an inspiration to see Rowenna’s genuine care and compassion for the communities and groups across Croydon. Our success is contingent on these groups coming together. Her policies offer the bold change that our borough deserves, and with Rowenna, we can get our town centre back!

Mark has lived in Croydon for 16 years. He works as a Librarian in an inner London mixed secondary school and is passionate about making our local schools as good as they can be. Having worked in public libraries in several London boroughs, he will support the Public Libraries used by Shirley's residents against Labour's threats of closures and cuts.

As a former Chairman and Vice Chairman of Croydon Community Groups, he will support our Residents Associations and the local community as a whole. He will also work hard to protect our green spaces from Labour development and help protect family homes from being turned into flats.

Shirley North should be a place where people feel safe, take pride in where they live, and see their area properly supported — with council tax spent where it makes a real difference.
From speaking to residents, it’s clear that people want safer streets, well-maintained public spaces and a stronger sense of community — both locally and across Croydon, including a town centre that feels safer, more welcoming and better managed, without Shirley North being overlooked.

I run a Croydon-based design practice and
bring experience delivering large-scale projects in the UK and internationally. My work has involved managing budgets, coordinating teams and delivering complex projects — giving me a practical understanding of how to turn ideas into real improvements.

My focus is on improving wellbeing and day-to-day life: safer, better-managed streets; real investment in green spaces and the high street; and housing that raises standards while protecting what people value — creating a place where everyone, including women, children and older residents, feels secure and supported.

I’m standing to listen, represent and deliver real improvements for local residents.

Drawing on my experience delivering complex projects, my priorities are:

1. Safer Communities, Tackling Crime
Cracking down on crime and anti-social behaviour while improving everyday safety across streets, public spaces and the town centre — so residents feel secure and confident where they live.

2. Pride in Place, Putting Residents First
Investing in green spaces, public areas and the high street to create well-designed, usable places — with better facilities and spaces that naturally bring people together.

3. Better Housing, Higher Standards
Improving housing quality, tackling poor conditions, and supporting well-designed homes that make best use of space, deliver real value, and respect the character of the area.

I am standing as a Reform UK Ward Champion for Shirley North because I care deeply about the future of our borough and believe local residents deserve better from their council. I want to see Croydon well managed, financially responsible, and focused on the everyday concerns of the people who live and work here.

Both nationally and locally, the UK is in need of positive change.

I grew up in the Borough of Croydon and have spent most of my adult life living here. I am a family man and work in legal services, and like many residents I want to see our area thrive – with safe streets, reliable services, and opportunities for future generations.

Over recent years, Croydon has faced real challenges. Residents see the impact in day-to-day life: pressure on services, rising council tax, and difficult financial decisions that have affected confidence in local government. Many people feel the council lacks a clear long-term plan and stronger financial discipline.

Local government should always put residents first. Too often, people feel decisions are made without proper engagement or a clear focus on delivery. It’s understandable that residents and local businesses feel they are paying more while receiving less in return.

Reform UK believes in sensible politics and professional management, both locally and nationally. At council level, that means living within our means, focusing on core services, delivering value for money, and being open, transparent and accountable to residents.

Croydon has real potential. London should be a thriving, booming global city of culture, creativity and opportunity – and Croydon should fully share in that success.

I am standing to help restore confidence, competence and community pride, and to ensure local government works better for the people who live, work and pay their taxes here.

Mark has lived at his home in Shirley North for over 13 years and started a family here. Both his sons have attended Monks Orchard Primary, where he also served as a school governor for many years. Living, commuting and socialising within the ward has given him a strong understanding of what makes Shirley North special, as well as where improvements are needed.

In Croydon, Ellie will work for:

• Safer roads in Shirley North, with better lighting and safe pedestrian crossings
• Investment in affordable and flexible childcare and better SEND services
• Housing for people not developers, that is truly affordable
• Improving Croydon town centre without pricing out local businesses and residents
• Removing barriers for disabled people
• An end to hate crime, domestic abuse and violence against women and girls

Ellie has lived in Croydon for almost 9 years and has family who have lived in the area for over 20. Ellie grew up visiting Croydon regularly for shopping and leisure. Ellie has been a campaigner for equality and inclusion for years. Working at a global animal welfare charity, Ellie is co-chair of the organisation’s LGBTQIA+ Network and works on the Equality, Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging working group. Ellie advocates for all her colleagues as Branch Equalities Officer of the trade union, as well as being part of a number of committees for the London and East of England region. Furthermore, Ellie volunteers at a UK wide LGBTQIA+ listening charity, directing members of the community, their friends, and families to support and resources.

“Fearmongering, division, and hate are rife in national politics, and the need for the Equality Party has never been more pertinent. Croydon needs councillors who will work for residents, and listen to their concerns to build a Croydon that’s for everyone.

“I’m someone who will speak up for those who have no voice, or who are consistently unheard and ignored. I listen to what people have to say, what they want to change, and what matters to them. I am invested in and care about people, their interests, and concerns. I’m excited to be representing the Equality Party and will bring my passion for advocacy and campaigning to deliver for the people of Croydon.”

A local Councillor in Croydon for 16 years Jason has worked hard in the local community, supporting residents and fighting to protect the character of Shirley against inappropriate development and to protect our Green spaces from Sadiq Khan.

A Croydon resident for over 30 years Jason had his wedding reception on Shirley Hills and raised his two children locally.

My name is Paula, and I have been part of the Croydon community since 2017, when I took over a local pub in Addiscombe. I joined Reform because I share their goals and their commitment to improving the local business environment, supporting small businesses, and standing up for the working class.

The hospitality sector is being hit hard. Rising taxes and soaring bills have created a volatile and unpredictable business climate, making it increasingly difficult for us to do our jobs. It’s easy to point out everything that’s going wrong, but I believe the time has come for us to take action. By addressing issues at a local level, we can help drive positive change across the country as a whole.

I am interested in becoming a local councillor because this role would give me the opportunity to work directly with the people and organisations who can help us tackle community challenges and build a better environment for everyone living in Croydon.

I look forward to representing the community of Shirley South, and I am confident that, together, we can make Croydon a better place and bring more people on board who believe in our goals and what we stand for.

I’m Antony, by day, a Solicitor of the Senior Courts of England and I grew up in this beautiful borough. Like many residents in Shirley, I’ve watched Croydon decline after years of mismanagement by Labour and Conservative career politicians. Croydon’s Reform offers a practical alternative to their chaos, focused on accountability and common sense. Our borough has faced bankruptcies, normalised crime and littering, stalled projects like Westfield and the East Croydon “bridge to nowhere”, even winters without bus stop shelters! Drivers in particular have faced unlawful LTNs, blanket 20mph limits, over-engineered cycle lanes and dodgy yellow box junctions. I’m standing to re-focus Croydon on the basics: cleaner streets, a safer town centre, and a sensible transport and planning policy.

Danielle Denton is your Local Conservative Candidate for South Croydon in the Council by-election.

South Croydon really matters to her. She has worked in South Croydon for over 30 years, and was a founding member of the South Croydon Business Association. She currently runs her own local business, and she is passionate about helping small businesses like those in South Croydon to thrive.

Danielle wants to work with Maria Gatland, Michael Neal, and Mayor Jason Perry, to restore pride in our community. She will work to restore the Graffiti Removal Team, reform planning, and get the grass in our parks cut.

I want to regenerate and rebuild and protect Croydon. I have chosen to stand in South Croydon ward as I live here and I would love the opportunity to do more to support the local community.

I would work diligently with all relevant authorities and organisations to make South Croydon safer for everyone, particularly women and girls. 
I have already met with local police officers to raise residents’ concerns and will continue to do so.

I have worked in different local authorities for 20 years, so I know how to work effectively through bureaucracy to get things done for residents.

I have real-world experience of regenerating high streets in other London boroughs. I would put my skills to use in my ward, especially our South End to unlock its potential.

I am dedicated to putting people first. As a volunteer for Nightwatch I have seen first-hand how housing shortages, mental health service delays and failures in adult social care impact people, with knock- on effects across the borough.

I have experience in working alongside a range of council services.  This knowledge combined with my legal background, means I would bring compassion, grit and the ability to get things done as a South Croydon Councillor.

I’ve spent years overseeing elections at St Andrew’s Church, Southbridge Road, making sure everything is run properly and fairly. Seeing South Croydon let down again by the same old parties, has made me step out from behind the desk to do something about it.

I am a local dad, and want the best for everyones future. I have spent my career in community development and housing, so I know how decisions at council level affect everyday life.

A keen parkrunner, cyclist, and conservation volunteer, I care deeply about our local heritage and green spaces. I will fight for clean and vibrant streets, fair housing, and an NHS which is properly protected.

South Croydon deserves a representative who knows the area, understands the pressures people are under and is prepared to speak up for them.

Benji is a local teacher, lecturer and theatre director. He has lived in Croydon for 13 years, and is proud to be standing to represent the people of South Croydon. He is a passionate advocate for the arts, and is devoted to making Croydon a hive of creativity and opportunity which can be enjoyed by the whole community. His experience leading large-scale companies around the UK, as well as working with community groups, youth theatres, schools and universities, means he is very well placed to instigate conversations to inspire and further the reach and range of culture for the ward and borough.

I am a legal immigrant, a classical liberal, and a patriot by choice who believes that Britain’s strength lies in its values, not its identity politics. My career in the technology sector has been dedicated to one thing: efficiency. As an Agile Programme Manager and practitioner of Lean Six Sigma, I specialise in optimising complex systems and ruthlessly eliminating waste.

I apply that same analytical rigour to politics. In Reform, I see a movement demanding common sense. They are the only party brave enough to judge individuals by the content of their character rather than the colour of their skin or the quotas they fill. My own journey to this country was driven by a respect for British law and liberty—ideals that are currently threatened by weak borders, wasteful spending, and a policing system afraid to enforce order.

I stand for an energy policy that prioritizes our sovereignty over expensive dogma, a tax system that rewards hard work rather than punishing success, and a return to focused, impartial policing. We must reject the soft bigotry of stereotyping and embrace a fierce meritocracy where freedom and security go hand in hand. I am here to fight for a future where this country is safe, prosperous, and unapologetically free.

Andrew has been self-employed in the music industry for over 25 years, establishing the musicians' collective London City Music Associates to oversee original music scoring for film and television.

Andrew specialises in music for film, TV and theatre, and has worked on film scores including _Shakespeare In Love_, _Billy Elliott_, and _Interstellar Live_, to West End shows. Andrew has lived in South Croydon since 1997 and sees the May 7th Croydon Council election as an opportunity to give back to his community with a particular focus on unlocking the potential of our wonderful neighbourhood - and our borough-wide cultural assets such as Fairfield Halls - making lives better for all who live and work here.

Andrew has volunteered for St John Ambulance as a Volunteer Vaccinator during the Covid-19 pandemic, as a GoodSam Community Responder for the Royal Voluntary Service and also works for the buildings preservation charity, The Landmark Trust.

Croydon is my home town. It sometimes gets a bad rap but there's tremendous community spirit and creativity. After the riots and in lockdowns, we didn't wait for authorities to grind into action; we hit the ground running with volunteers and local mutual aid groups.
That 'Can do' attitude gets things done and makes life better for everyone. Whether I'm whipping up a vegan feast or getting what a local resident deserves from their gas, electricity or water supplier, it gives me a buzz to help others. That's why I was a Scout leader for several years. Fighting for my late mum was tough (as her primary carer whilst bringing up my own children); fortunately I had support from my partner but others aren't so lucky.
South Norwood has great diversity, history and creativity - a real melting pot of cultures and cuisines. It's where I learned to swim, where I socialise and shop and much more. It's time to show the unloved spots some love, show people we care and realise some of the huge potential.

I have been campaigning for years for safer streets especially for women and girls, as a councillor I will work tirelessly for a safer, cleaner and fairer South Norwood.

Originally from Europe I have been welcomed in Croydon and made it my home.

South Norwood offers the best of both worlds: beautiful green spaces, brilliant transport connections, and a growing community of local businesses.

But there are things in South Norwood that must change.
As a councillor I will make sure we keep maintaining our amazing green spaces and take real action against those who pollute them.

I know many residents in South Norwood are not being treated fairly. No one should have to suffer damp and mould or poor quality but expensive housing.

As a trustee of a local law centre that supports residents on low and no incomes, I have the experience to resolve problems quickly and efficiently.

I am committed to supporting our hardworking community groups and making sure everyone in South Norwood has a voice.

It’s time for real change. If you care about safe neighborhoods, affordable housing, thriving high streets, and fair council taxes—it’s time to vote for leadership that actually listens to YOU.

Meet your TTIP Mayoral Candidate, Michael Pusey MBE. He’s ready to fight for our community, restore pride to our streets, and bring total transparency back to local government. Plus, he's an all-around nice guy! 🤝💜

Croydon’s Tory mayor has increased council tax
by 33% since 2023. Every council service has faced
cuts, job losses, and austerity. How many more politicians will vote for more devastating cuts and council tax increases to make up for the loss of central government funding?

In 2024, Labour promised change but all we got was the same old cruel Tory policies. They all say “better our cuts than their cuts” and continue to make us pay for the system’s crisis. They put the interests of the wealthy 1% elite first. They give contracts to private property developers rather than implementing a mass programme of council house building and introducing rent controls.

Despite voicing opposition, the Greens don’t go far enough to stand up to Starmer’s austerity. They also have a bad record wherever they have been in power. We want to work with any Green candidate who pledges not to make any cut

But we need real anti-austerity councillors who will vote for no-cuts needs budgets and campaign with our communities and trade unions to win back the millions stolen from our borough. Councils can fight cuts and twist the government’s arm into paying up, instead of forcing workers to pick up the bill.

As a member of the Socialist Party, I am standing as part of the Trade Unionist and Socialist Coalition to fight exactly for that. As a trade unionist, I argue for a new party based on the working class. A party that will take on the establishment parties and put forward a socialist alternative to war and austerity.
