VOTE FOR A SOCIALIST MAYOR WHO WILL FIGHT FOR THE MONEY AND RESOURCES CROYDON NEEDS!

WE NEED A BUDGET BASED ON OUR NEEDS NOT CUTS!

1) No more council tax hikes! Don’t make us pay to make up for the loss of central government funding.

2) Reverse Tory-Labour cuts. For a no-cuts needs-based
budget and building a campaign to win the money Croydon needs. Demand the money from the Labour government.

3) End the cycle of knife crime - campaign for jobs and services for young peop

My name is Jose Piedade Fernandes and I'm standing to be councillor candidate in Thornton Heath. I'm Croydon resident since 2002 and living with my family. 
I believe in strong family values,  and families deserves to live safely and securely, needs support, but this is not the case in Thornton Heath. 
On the campaign trail I have come across issues such as housing, ward filled wth fly-tips, high Street business struggling. This stories has strengthened my determination and I have stepped forward to stand for people to make sure that local communities has hope.

As a Management Accountant, Auditor and current  Parent Governor, committed to do things right. These professional expertises will allow me to resolve issues and commitment to Thornton Heath communities.

​I am Vicky Ekua Newton , a property management consultant, Community Leader and UN Peace Ambassador. My bond with Thornton Heath is lifelong; from managing the after-school club at Winterbourne Girls School to my years at a local estate agency, I have always been deeply woven into the fabric of this neighborhood.
​Through my professional work as a property management Consultant, I witnessed  the direct impact of the housing crisis daily, which drives my dedication to helping residents secure decent affordable homes. My commitment to service extends from volunteering for an adults' learning disability charity to supporting local entrepreneurship, such as the family-run hair salon on our High Street which also provides jobs for residents. I understand the challenges our local businesses face because I have been in the trenches with them. Thornton Heath boasts of vibrant businesses, voluntary sector, wonderful green spaces like Grange wood park and Whitehorse Meadow .  I would like the opportunity to serve and be a visible, accessible representative, ensuring our high streets thrives, affordable and decent homes , our streets remain safe and clean, and our young people receive the opportunities they deserve. 
Vote Vicky 4 ThorntonHeath .

My name is Benjamin Justin Lewis Taylor (or Ben) I live in Croydon with my wife where we moved several years ago to start our family.

I wasn’t born into a life of privilege, politics for me is about the struggle for justice not a comfortable career. I was raised by a single mum on benefits so I know what it’s like to see a parent worry about the bills, to live in temporary accommodation not knowing how long you’ll be there.

I also know what it’s like to be ambitious and work towards aspirational goals. I was very lucky to come of age under a Labour government, it meant doors were open for me that were closed for years do many people.

I am Jamie Ahinasi-Blondy, and I am honoured to stand as a Reform UK Ward Champion for Thornton Heath ward.

My mission is simple: to bring genuine common-sense and professional integrity back to our local governance. My commitment to this borough is profound, built on years of living and serving here, particularly in the communities stretching from Thornton Heath to Norwood Junction.

As I look at the state of our Council, I am driven by this conviction:

“I stand because I believe our community deserves strong, practical representation that puts local people first. Having lived in Croydon for nine years, I have seen both the strengths of our borough and the areas where residents feel let down. My service through the church food bank during the pandemic showed me the resilience of our community but also highlighted the urgent need for policies that address everyday struggles such as cost of living, housing, and local services. Reform UK’s focus on accountability, fairness, and common-sense solutions resonates with my own values. I want to ensure that Croydon residents have a voice that challenges waste, prioritises local needs, and works to restore trust in local government. My commitment is to stand up for ordinary people, to listen carefully, and to act decisively on the issues that matter most to our community.”

That commitment to decisive action is backed by professional competence. For years, I have worked as a Senior Risk Assurance Manager, holding high-level public sector programmes to account. I am a certified expert in governance and fiscal integrity. When I look at the Council’s finances, I don’t see a political problem; I see an assurance failure that my skills are placed to fix.

I will bring that rigour to the Council chamber. I will use my expertise as a Data Analyst to expose waste and drive process optimisation, ensuring we maximise service delivery while reducing the burden on our local ratepayers. My job will be to cut the bureaucracy a

I believe that our residents deserve a better service from this Council, fair allocation of resources and to see that this council functions for the benefit of all the people of Croydon. I wish to represent this amazing ward and the community of people within it, and put the Council under scrutiny for their financial mismanagement and failure to ensure that Thornton Heath thrives.

Mel Mullings: A Train Driver, Not a Career Politician. 'No cuts' socialist candidate

I am explicitly socialist. A community organiser with a proven track record of standing up for Croydon, EVERYTIME! I am a London Underground train driver with 25 years as a militant organiser in the RMT union. I stand unapologetically for Palestine, Congo and anywhere there's oppression. I am anti-racist, anti fascist, anti war and intersectional activist. I am not standing as a local councillor to "manage" our decline or "lobby" Westminster for crumbs. I am standing to lead a working-class takeover of our local economy.

Thornton Heath is being bled dry. While our libraries close and fly-tipping piles up on Brigstock Road, Croydon Council is funnelling £86 million a year in interest payments to City of London bankers. That is an "Interest Tax" on the poor. I am the only candidate with a plan to stop this theft.

My Radical Pledges:

Financial Sovereignty, Not Begging: I will not wait for a "fair deal" from the government. I will fight to establish a Public Credit Facility to refinance our debt at 0–1% interest. We stop paying the bankers and start paying for our parks, roads, and youth services.

Housing is a Right, Not a Market: The current system "licenses" bad landlords; I want to replace them. We will treat housing as a public utility like water. No more "mega-developers" building luxury flats on Bensham Grove while 8,000 families wait for homes. We build for need, at cost, for the many.

Seize the High Street: Stagnant sites like Ambassador House are monuments to failed capitalism. I will use the council’s powers to take back empty shops and turn them into Common Wealth Zones giving local entrepreneurs free or at-cost space, energy, and internet.

Reparations as Labour Rights: As a Black woman with an invisible disability, I know that "equality" is a hollow word without Restorative Justice. For me, reparations isn't just history; it’s also the modern struggle to return power,

Simon is a retailer by profession and has lived in Croydon since 2019. Waddon residents have been ignored for too long and Simon is passionate about making their voices heard and making sure results are delivered. Simon is determined to work hard for all Waddon residents, tackle anti social behaviour and deliver sensible planning. Waddon is a special place in Croydon and deserves a council that works for them!

I have lived in Croydon for over 16 years and in Waddon for nine of those. I live with my young family and we use the local facilities where available- surgery, play group, cafe, motor mechanic and church. We are active members of St Dominic and St Mary's Catholic Churches and attend activities in others like St George's church. I am a local pharmacist manager and have helped in delivery of healthcare to local residents for about 15 years. I am passionate about family and community engagement. His priorities include tackling local crime, dealing with waste collection, and making our parks safer for children to play by getting the grass cut and the local pond cleared.

Rym has worked for the local MP Sarah as a caseworker for three years. She is a strong believer that politicians should be held accountable as they work for the people; the people do not work for them.
Through her work, she has seen firsthand the issues that the community faces. Rym calls for effective representation of local voices and hopes to do so if elected

Amy lives with her young family in South Norwood and has a background in teaching and the charity sector. She's a community champion and as a Councillor has worked on projects making Woodside and South Norwood a better place to live.

I am a local standing for Reform UK in Croydon, a former Army serviceman shaped by the values of discipline, duty, and service. Guided by a stoic mindset and grounded in my Christian faith, I believe in resilience, personal responsibility, and facing difficult realities with honesty rather than empty promises.

I am proud of my mixed English and Sri Lankan heritage, which has given me a deep respect for community, family, and shared values. As a Croydon resident, parent, and working individual, I understand the everyday pressures facing local people — the rising cost of living, housing challenges, public safety concerns, and the need for a council that spends taxpayers’ money wisely and transparently.

Croydon is a borough full of hardworking people and strong communities, yet it has too often been failed by poor leadership, financial mismanagement, and decisions made without proper accountability. Drawing on my military experience, my faith, and a belief in common sense over ideology, I am standing for Reform UK to challenge the status quo and put local residents first.

We support safer neighbourhoods, cleaner streets, responsible use of council funds, and a return to order, fairness, and accountability in local government. We believe leadership is not about slogans, virtue-signalling, or empty rhetoric, but about integrity, discipline, and doing what is right — even when it is not easy.

Above all, I believe real change starts locally: by listening to residents, standing firm on principles, and working to ensure Croydon is governed with seriousness, honesty, and genuine respect for the people who live here.
